verses.1: A Shiggaion of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning Cush, a Benjaminite. O LORD my God, in You I have taken refuge; Save me from all those who pursue me, and rescue me,
verses.2: Or he will tear my soul like a lion, Dragging me away, while there is no one to rescue me.
verses.3: O LORD my God, if I have done this, If there is injustice in my hands,
verses.4: If I have done evil to my friend, Or have plundered my enemy for no reason,
verses.5: Let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it; And let him trample my life to the ground And lay my glory in the dust. Selah
verses.6: Arise, LORD, in Your anger; Raise Yourself against the rage of my enemies, And stir Yourself for me; You have ordered judgment.
verses.7: Let the assembly of the peoples encompass You, And return on high over it.
verses.8: The LORD judges the peoples; Vindicate me, LORD, according to my righteousness and my integrity that is in me.
verses.9: Please let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; For the righteous God puts hearts and minds to the test.
verses.10: My shield is with God, Who saves the upright in heart.
verses.11: God is a righteous judge, And a God who shows indignation every day.
verses.12: If one does not repent, He will sharpen His sword; He has bent His bow and taken aim.
verses.13: He has also prepared deadly weapons for Himself; He makes His arrows fiery shafts.
verses.14: Behold, an evil person is pregnant with injustice, And he conceives harm and gives birth to lies.
verses.15: He has dug a pit and hollowed it out, And has fallen into the hole which he made.
verses.16: His harm will return on his own head, And his violence will descend on the top of his own head.
verses.17: I will give thanks to the LORD according to His righteousness And will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.
